series_metadata.series_description: records submitted by insurance companies providing notification to or requesting approval of the Department of Insurance for specific transactions; includes forms, correspondence, and other related records

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Resolution PLUS: 10 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: work papers directly pertaining to audits by the Office of the State Auditor

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 10 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: final reports, including conclusions or recommendations, created by the Office of the State Auditor in the conduct of financial, performance, investigative, or information system audits

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center when reference value ends for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: final reports, including conclusions or recommendations, related to internal compliance or operational audits, hazardous material, or those that document a significant change in agency practices or have significant administrative value

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center when reference value ends for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: final reports, including conclusions or recommendations related to internal accounting systems and controls, administrative systems and controls, information technology systems and controls, skills, or those with limited administrative value

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 10 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: work papers and other evidence and related supportive material directly pertaining to audits
tax audits conducted by the Department of Revenue on the International Fuel Tax Association, Inc. (IFTA) and the International Registration Plan, Inc. (IRP)

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 6 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: remaining tax audits conducted by the Department of Revenue

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 4 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_title: Consultant, Contractor, Provider, and Vendor Due Diligence Records
series_metadata.series_description: records documenting the evaluation of consultants, contractors, providers, and vendors with whom the agency conducts business; includes financial stability, information security risk assessments, and other related records SEE ALSO: Contracts (LEGAL); Discrimination Complaint Investigations (LEGAL); Grants Management (FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT)

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Contract expires PLUS: 2 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_title: Consultant, Contractor, Provider, and Vendor Due Diligence Records
series_metadata.series_description: records documenting the vetting of contractors and vendors with whom the agency considers conducting business but does not award a contract SEE ALSO: Contracts (LEGAL); Discrimination Complaint Investigations (LEGAL); Grants Management (FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT)

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 1 year THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: records documenting agency actions to address concerns raised by an audit or complaint

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Effective PLUS: 2 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning investigations triggered by questions about ethics or conduct within an agency, such as whistleblower reports or allegations of fraud; includes complaints, reports, investigations, and other related records SEE ALSO: Investigation and Hearing Records; Grievances (HUMAN RESOURCES) SEE ALSO: Ombudsmen (GOVERNANCE)

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Resolution PLUS: 3 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_title: Rates and Standards (continued on following page)
series_metadata.series_description: records concerning tests conducted to establish official samples, weights and measures, and other standards; also includes rate setting documentation

records concerning quasi-judicial hearings and rulings by the Commissioner of Insurance regarding rate filings; includes official court records, dockets, pleadings, testimony, transcripts, and other related records

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Resolution PLUS: 20 years ΩTHEN: Destroy*

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center 10 years after case closes. Records will be held for agency 10 additional years and then destroyed.

series_metadata.series_description: investigations by the Office of the State Auditor that substantiate allegations received regarding improper governmental conduct, violation of State or federal law, or dangers posed to public health and safety

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Determination THEN: Transfer to Audit Reports NOTE: Pursuant to G.S. § 147-64.6B(b), when the Auditor believes that an allegation of improper governmental activity is outside the authority set forth in G.S. § 147-64.6, the Auditor shall refer the allegation to the appropriate State agency responsible for the enforcement or administration of the matter for investigation

series_metadata.series_description: allegations of improper governmental activities that the Office of the State Auditor finds unsubstantiated

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Receipt PLUS: 3 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: guaranty bonds, surety bonds, and any other collateral securities required for licensing or performance of service

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Expiration or cancellation PLUS: 5 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning individuals or entities whose initial application is denied; includes reports, decisions, correspondence, and other related records

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Expiration of application period PLUS: 2 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: records documenting the effects of proposed activities by a contractor or provider on the environment

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center when reference value ends for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: applications that are not completed within the window of time provided by the agency or that are withdrawn from consideration

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Expiration of application period or withdrawal PLUS: 1 year THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: examinations and background checks related to renewable accreditations, licenses, registrations, and permits

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Reference value ends ± THEN: Destroy Agency Policy: ________________

series_metadata.series_title: records concerning lifetime or perpetual authorizations
series_metadata.series_description: RETAIN UNTIL: Resignation from license, death of holder, or dissolution of entity PLUS: 1 year THEN: Destroy
series_metadata.legal_citation: source documentation collected by the Division of Motor Vehicles in order to issue Real ID cards

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning lifetime professional licenses; also includes Certificate of Need documentation for healthcare facilities

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Received PLUS: 75 years ΩTHEN: Destroy

tracking_data.comments: Transfer records to the State Records Center after 6 months. Records will be held for agency 75 additional years and then destroyed.

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning authorizations that impact the general public. Includes licenses and permits for radioactive materials and solid waste, as well as corporation charters

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center when reference value ends for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: records compiled by the Department of Health and Human Services concerning certification for lead abatement and lead renovation work; includes permits, notifications, inspection reports, enforcement actions, and other related records

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Received PLUS: 22 years ΩTHEN: Destroy

tracking_data.comments: Transfer records to the State Records Center after 3 years. Records will be held for agency 19 additional years and then destroyed.

series_metadata.series_description: listings of entities and individuals authorized to engage in a particular occupation or activity; includes applications, evaluations, and other related records necessary to generate the registry

records concerning registrations with the Department of the Secretary of State; includes trademarks, labels, and designs as well as an index of lobbyists, lobbyist principals, solicitors, and state and local government liaisons

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center when reference value ends for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning individuals or entities whose license, permit, or other authorization is revoked for cause; includes reports, decisions, correspondence, and other related records

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Revocation PLUS: 50 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning licenses or permits revoked due to clerical error; also includes records of revocations that are lifted due to an exoneration of the cause for revocation

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Revocation PLUS: 1 year THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: agency certifications of training opportunities to be provided by outside entities
records accumulated by the Department of Health and Human Services concerning prescriptions for all Schedule II through V controlled substances
series_metadata.legal_citation: Authority G.S. § 90-113.73 Confidentiality G.S. § 90-113.74(a) Retention G.S. § 90-113.74(f)

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Received PLUS: 6 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: records of monitoring and inspections that have value for documenting patterns SEE ALSO: Inspections (RISK MANAGEMENT)

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center when reference value ends for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: records documenting routine, random, and requested evaluations and inspections; includes interviews, evidence, reports, examinations, notices of violations, audits, recommendations, correspondence, and other related records; also includes samples testing SEE ALSO: Inspections (RISK MANAGEMENT)

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 3 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning complaints, fraud allegations, contestments, grievances, and inquiries against individuals and other entities; includes summaries, charges, reports, assessments and findings, hearing transcripts and evidence, decisions and sanctions, referrals, correspondence, and other related records SEE ALSO: Improper Conduct Investigations; Law Enforcement Case Records (LAW ENFORCEMENT); Legal Case Records, OAH Case Records, Other Administrative Hearings (LEGAL) (continued on following page)

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 3 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning complaints against the conduct, character, or fitness of elected or appointed officials that are investigated by an external entity; includes statements, correspondence, recommendations, and other related records; also includes cases that result in discipline by the North Carolina State Bar Disciplinary Hearing Commission or by the courts or disbarment by the Council of the North Carolina State Bar SEE ALSO: Improper Conduct Investigations; Law Enforcement Case Records (LAW ENFORCEMENT); Legal Case Records, OAH Case Records, Other Administrative Hearings (LEGAL) (continued on following page)

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 50 years ΩTHEN: Destroy

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center after 5 years. Records will be held for agency 45 additional years and then destroyed.

series_metadata.series_description: work papers for fraud investigations by the Department of State Treasurer SEE ALSO: Improper Conduct Investigations; Law Enforcement Case Records (LAW ENFORCEMENT); Legal Case Records, OAH Case Records, Other Administrative Hearings (LEGAL) (continued on following page)

records concerning rate cases heard by the North Carolina Utilities Commission; includes applications, orders, transcripts, exhibits, complaints, and other related records SEE ALSO: Improper Conduct Investigations; Law Enforcement Case Records (LAW ENFORCEMENT); Legal Case Records, OAH Case Records, Other Administrative Hearings (LEGAL) (continued on following page)

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 75 years ΩTHEN: Destroy

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center 2 years after closing. Records will be held for agency 73 additional years and then destroyed.

series_metadata.series_description: transcripts of hearing proceedings of the State Board of Elections SEE ALSO: Improper Conduct Investigations; Law Enforcement Case Records (LAW ENFORCEMENT); Legal Case Records, OAH Case Records, Other Administrative Hearings (LEGAL) (continued on following page)

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center when reference value ends for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning the monitoring of individuals, entities, and processes SEE ALSO: (above); Grants Management (FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT); External Surveys (PUBLIC RELATIONS); Employee Suggestions and Surveys (HUMAN RESOURCES); Health Monitoring Programs (HEALTHCARE) Contractor, Provider, and Vendor Due Diligence Records

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 3 years THEN: Destroy
